---
title: "EDA: Análise da concentração de Silica como série temporal"
author: "Hanna Tatsuta Galassi"
date: "2026-01-15"
format: html
toc: true
echo: false
standalone: true
---

```{python}
from datetime import date, timedelta, datetime
import os
from typing import Optional, Tuple

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
```

```{python}
path_df = "../data/interim/MiningProcess_Flotation_Plant_Database.csv"
df_data = pd.read_csv(
    path_df, index_col="date_fixed", parse_dates=["date_fixed"]
)
```

## Visualização dos dados importados

```{python}
df_data.head()
```

# Ajuste de amostragem

As medições de laboratório na corrente de concentrado são realizadas a cada 1h.

Já na corrente de alimentação, são realizadas a cada 9 horas. 

```{python}
cols_9h = [
    "% Iron Feed",
    "% Silica Feed"]
cols_1h = [
    "% Iron Concentrate",
    "% Silica Concentrate"
]
cols_24h = cols_1h + cols_9h

df_1h_resampled = df_data[cols_1h].resample("1h").first()
df_9h_resampled = df_data[cols_9h].resample("9h").first()
df_24h_resampled = df_data[cols_24h].resample("1d").mean()
```

Ao fazer a reamostragem para a frequência de 1 horas, ficamos com **4415** amostras da variável de interesse, a concentração de Silica no concentrado.


# Visualização das variáveis reamostradas

## Corrente de concentrado (amostrada a cada 1 hora)

```{python}
df_plot = df_1h_resampled

data_start = str(df_data.index.min())
data_end = str(df_data.index.max())

plot_start = data_start #'2017-05-01 00:00:00' #
plot_end = data_end #'2017-05-02 00:00:00'

df_plot = df_plot[((df_plot.index > plot_start) & 
                   (df_plot.index < plot_end))]
variables_to_plot = cols_1h
```

```{python}
for var in variables_to_plot:
    if var not in df_plot.columns:
        print(f"Warning: '{var}' not found in DataFrame.")
        continue

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_plot.index,
        y=df_plot[var],
        mode='lines',
        line=dict(color='#023e56'),
        name=var
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title=var,
        xaxis_title='Index',
        yaxis_title=var,
        template='plotly_white',
        width=800,
        height=675
    )

    fig.show()
```


## Corrente de alimentação (amostrada a cada 9 horas)

```{python}
df_plot = df_9h_resampled

plot_start = data_start #'2017-05-01 00:00:00' #
plot_end = data_end #'2017-05-02 00:00:00'

df_plot = df_plot[((df_plot.index > plot_start) & 
                   (df_plot.index < plot_end))]
variables_to_plot = cols_9h
```

```{python}
for var in variables_to_plot:
    if var not in df_plot.columns:
        print(f"Warning: '{var}' not found in DataFrame.")
        continue

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_plot.index,
        y=df_plot[var],
        mode='lines',
        line=dict(color='#023e56'),
        name=var
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title=var,
        xaxis_title='Index',
        yaxis_title=var,
        template='plotly_white',
        width=800,
        height=675
    )

    fig.show()
```

## Reamostragem diária

```{python}
df_plot = df_24h_resampled

plot_start = data_start #'2017-05-01 00:00:00' #
plot_end = data_end #'2017-05-02 00:00:00'

df_plot = df_plot[((df_plot.index > plot_start) & 
                   (df_plot.index < plot_end))]
variables_to_plot = cols_24h
```

```{python}
for var in variables_to_plot:
    if var not in df_plot.columns:
        print(f"Warning: '{var}' not found in DataFrame.")
        continue

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_plot.index,
        y=df_plot[var],
        mode='lines',
        line=dict(color='#023e56'),
        name=var
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title=var,
        xaxis_title='Index',
        yaxis_title=var,
        template='plotly_white',
        width=800,
        height=675
    )

    fig.show()
```

# Busca por períodos contínuos

```{python}
def find_longest_continuous_segment(
    series: pd.Series,
    expected_freq: Optional[pd.Timedelta] = None
) -> Tuple[pd.Series, pd.Timestamp, pd.Timestamp]:
    """Find the longest continuous segment in a time series.

    A "continuous" segment is defined as a sequence of non-null values where
    the time gaps between consecutive timestamps do not exceed a given
    expected frequency.

    If expected_freq is not provided, the function will infer it from the
    most common time delta in the index.

    Args:
        series: Pandas Series with a DatetimeIndex.
        expected_freq: Optional expected time frequency as a pandas.Timedelta.
            If None, it will be inferred from the index differences.

    Returns:
        A tuple containing:
            - longest_segment: The longest continuous segment as a Series.
            - longest_start: The start timestamp of the longest segment.
            - longest_end: The end timestamp of the longest segment.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the series is empty, has a non-datetime index, or if
            the frequency cannot be inferred.
    """
    if series is None or len(series) == 0:
        raise ValueError("Input series is empty or None.")

    if not isinstance(series.index, pd.DatetimeIndex):
        raise ValueError(
            "Series index must be a DatetimeIndex."
        )

    # Remove leading and trailing NaNs but keep internal NaNs for segmentation
    series = series.copy()

    # Infer expected frequency if not provided
    if expected_freq is None:
        diffs = series.index.to_series().diff().dropna()
        if diffs.empty:
            raise ValueError(
                "Cannot infer frequency from a single timestamp."
            )
        # Use the most frequent time delta as the expected frequency
        expected_freq = diffs.mode().iloc[0]

    if not isinstance(expected_freq, pd.Timedelta):
        raise ValueError(
            "expected_freq must be a pandas.Timedelta instance."
        )

    longest_start: Optional[pd.Timestamp] = None
    longest_end: Optional[pd.Timestamp] = None
    current_start: Optional[pd.Timestamp] = None

    longest_length: int = 0

    prev_timestamp: Optional[pd.Timestamp] = None

    for timestamp, value in series.items():
        # Break if value is NaN (interrupts the segment)
        if pd.isna(value):
            if current_start is not None and prev_timestamp is not None:
                current_length = (
                    series.loc[current_start:prev_timestamp].shape[0]
                )
                if current_length > longest_length:
                    longest_length = current_length
                    longest_start = current_start
                    longest_end = prev_timestamp
            current_start = None
            prev_timestamp = None
            continue

        # Start of a new potential segment
        if current_start is None:
            current_start = timestamp
            prev_timestamp = timestamp
            continue

        # Check time gap
        if prev_timestamp is not None:
            gap = timestamp - prev_timestamp
            if gap > expected_freq:
                # Close current segment
                current_length = (
                    series.loc[current_start:prev_timestamp].shape[0]
                )
                if current_length > longest_length:
                    longest_length = current_length
                    longest_start = current_start
                    longest_end = prev_timestamp

                # Start a new segment at current timestamp
                current_start = timestamp

        prev_timestamp = timestamp

    # Check the last open segment, if any
    if current_start is not None and prev_timestamp is not None:
        current_length = series.loc[current_start:prev_timestamp].shape[0]
        if current_length > longest_length:
            longest_length = current_length
            longest_start = current_start
            longest_end = prev_timestamp

    if longest_start is None or longest_end is None:
        raise ValueError(
            "No valid continuous segment found in the series."
        )

    longest_segment = series.loc[longest_start:longest_end]

    return longest_segment, longest_start, longest_end
```

```{python}
target = "% Silica Concentrate"

longest_segment, longest_start, longest_end = find_longest_continuous_segment(
    df_1h_resampled[target]
)
```

```{python}
print(f"Maior segmento contínuo para '{target}':")
print(f"Início: {longest_start}")
print(f"Final: {longest_end}")
print(f"Comprimento: {longest_segment.shape[0]} samples")
```

```{python}
df_1h_resampled = df_1h_resampled.loc[longest_start:longest_end]
df_9h_resampled = df_9h_resampled.loc[longest_start:longest_end]
df_24h_resampled = df_24h_resampled.loc[longest_start:longest_end]
```

```{python}
df_1h_resampled.to_csv("../data/interim/resampled_1h.csv")
df_9h_resampled.to_csv("../data/interim/resampled_9h.csv")
df_24h_resampled.to_csv("../data/interim/resampled_24h.csv")
```
